finding: The repository had a tested continuity claim-graph validator, but no automatic repository gate required the committed continuity index to satisfy it after future changes.
memory_recovered: Contradictions and supersessions must remain additive linked records; repository automation should reject dangling, circular, self-referential, duplicate, or state-invalid claim histories before they merge.

observed: The continuity index contains explicit contradicts and supersedes relations.
The claim-graph module and deterministic tests existed before this run.
No continuity-specific CI workflow was found before this run.
A CLI validator and path-scoped GitHub Actions workflow were committed.
inferred: Running both contract fixtures and validation of the committed index should reduce silent corruption of project memory.
proposed: Require the Continuity claim graph integrity check before merging continuity-index changes.
superseded: Manual-only validation of contradiction and supersession relationships.
unresolved: The new workflow has not yet produced an observed successful run for its own commit.
The index still lacks an authentic contradiction-linked record pair.

verification.source_behavior: The CLI parses the selected JSON index, invokes validateClaimGraph, emits a structured report, and exits nonzero on graph or parse failure.
verification.workflow_behavior: The workflow runs deterministic tests, validates the committed index, and retains the JSON report even when the job fails.

falsification_route: Introduce a fixture or branch change containing a dangling relation, self-relation, duplicate id, invalid state, superseded record without predecessor, or supersession cycle; the workflow must fail and retain a report naming the corresponding error code.
